# 🧬 Comprehensive Research Report: `samtools` v1.23.1
**GenePattern Module Development Reference**
*Prepared by: Bioinformatics Research Analysis System*

---

1. [Tool Overview & Scientific Context](#1-tool-overview)
2. [Input/Output Formats & Requirements](#2-io-formats)

## 1. Tool Overview & Scientific Context

### 1.1 Identity

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Name** | SAMtools |
| **Version** | 1.23.1 |
| **Language** | C (compiled binary) |
| **License** | MIT (permissive open-source) |
| **Repository** | https://github.com/samtools/samtools |
| **Homepage** | http://www.htslib.org/ |
| **Organization** | samtools org (Wellcome Sanger Institute + community) |
| **Companion Library** | HTSlib (shared C library foundation) |
| **Primary Citation** | Li et al. (2009), *Bioinformatics* 25(16):2078–2079; PMID: **19505943** |

### 1.2 Primary Purpose

SAMtools is the **de facto reference implementation and standard toolkit** for post-processing, manipulating, and analyzing high-throughput sequencing (HTS) alignment data stored in **SAM, BAM, and CRAM** formats. Originally developed as part of the **1000 Genomes Project** infrastructure, it has become an indispensable component of virtually every NGS analysis pipeline in existence.

> *"SAMtools implements various utilities for post-processing alignments in the SAM format, such as indexing, variant caller and alignment viewer."* — Original publication abstract, 2009

### 1.3 Scientific Applications

SAMtools serves as the central preprocessing and format-conversion hub in essentially all genomics domains:

| Application Domain | SAMtools Role |
|---|---|
| Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) | Sort, index, deduplicate, QC, pileup |
| Whole Exome Sequencing (WES) | Targeted region extraction, depth, coverage |
| RNA-seq | BAM post-processing, QC statistics, format conversion |
| ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq / CUT&RUN | BAM filtering, deduplication, depth analysis |
| Single-cell Genomics | BAM manipulation, cell barcode tag operations |
| Amplicon Sequencing | Primer clipping, amplicon statistics |
| Long-read Sequencing (PacBio/ONT) | CRAM support, region extraction, statistics |
| Population Genomics | Multi-sample pileup, variant preprocessing |
| Metagenomics | Read filtering, unmapped read extraction |
| CNV Analysis | Coverage depth, pileup generation (see bcftools docs) |
| Forensic Genomics | Duplicate detection, alignment QC |

### 1.4 Ecosystem Role

SAMtools occupies the **critical middleware layer** in the NGS ecosystem:

```
[Aligners]         [SAMtools]            [Downstream Tools]
BWA-MEM   ──────►  view (convert)  ──►  GATK HaplotypeCaller
STAR      ──────►  sort            ──►  bcftools call
HISAT2    ──────►  index           ──►  featureCounts / HTSeq
minimap2  ──────►  fixmate         ──►  deeptools
bowtie2   ──────►  markdup         ──►  bedtools
                   mpileup         ──►  DESeq2 / edgeR
                   stats/flagstat  ──►  MultiQC (QC reporting)
                   faidx           ──►  IGV (visualization)
```

---

## 2. Input/Output Formats & Requirements

### 2.1 Primary Input Formats

#### SAM (Sequence Alignment/Map)
- **Type:** Human-readable tab-delimited text
- **Structure:** Header section (@HD, @SQ, @RG, @PG lines) + alignment records with **11 mandatory fields**: QNAME, FLAG, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, SEQ, QUAL
- **Size:** Typically large (uncompressed); 10–100 GB for human WGS
- **Use:** Primary output format from aligners; input for initial conversion

#### BAM (Binary Alignment/Map)
- **Type:** BGZF-compressed binary version of SAM
- **Compression:** DEFLATE-based BGZF (blocked gzip); random-access capable with index
- **Size:** ~3–5x smaller than equivalent SAM; typical human WGS ~30–80 GB
- **Index:** Requires `.bam.bai` (BAI) or `.bam.csi` (CSI) for random access
- **Limitation:** BAI format cannot index chromosomes > 2²⁹ bases (~512 Mb); use CSI for scaffolded assemblies

#### CRAM (Compressed Reference-oriented Alignment Map)
- **Type:** Reference-based compression; far more compact than BAM
- **Versions:** v2.1 (lossless), v3.0 (lossless + optional lossy), v3.1 (default in htslib ≥1.21; improved compression)
- **Size:** ~40–50% smaller than BAM; requires reference genome for decoding
- **Index:** Requires `.cram.crai` index
- **Important:** Default output CRAM version changed to 3.1 in htslib 1.21+; some older tools may not support 3.1

#### FASTA/FASTQ
- **Input to:** `faidx`, `fqidx`, `calmd`, `mpileup` (reference), `import`
- **Output from:** `fasta`, `fastq` conversion commands
- **bgzip support:** bgzip-compressed + tabix-indexed FASTA fully supported via htslib

#### BED (Browser Extensible Data)
- **Used by:** `view -L`, `bedcov`, `ampliconclip`, `depth -b`, `stats -t`
- **Format:** Tab-delimited: chrom, chromStart, chromEnd (0-based, half-open intervals)

#### VCF/BCF
- **Output from:** `mpileup -v` (VCF) / `mpileup -g` (BCF, deprecated)
- **Intended use:** Pipe to `bcftools call` for variant calling

### 2.2 Primary Output Formats

| Command | Primary Output | Secondary Output |
|---|---|---|
| `view` | SAM / BAM / CRAM | Filtered-out reads (--output-unselected) |
| `sort` | BAM / CRAM / SAM | Temp files during sort |
| `index` | `.bai` or `.csi` or `.crai` | — |
| `flagstat` | Text / JSON / TSV | — |
| `stats` | Text statistics file | Per-RG split files (-S) |
| `depth` | Tab-delimited text (chr, pos, depth) | — |
| `coverage` | Tab-delimited coverage table | — |
| `idxstats` | Tab-delimited (ref, len, mapped, unmapped) | — |
| `mpileup` | Pileup text / BCF / VCF | — |
| `faidx` | Sequence (stdout/file) + `.fai` index | — |
| `fastq` | FASTQ file(s) | — |
| `fasta` | FASTA file | — |
| `consensus` | FASTA / FASTQ / pileup | — |
| `markdup` | BAM + optional stats file | JSON stats (--json) |

### 2.3 SAM FLAG Field Values (Critical for Filtering)

| Bit Value | Hex | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 0x1 | Template having multiple segments (paired-end) |
| 2 | 0x2 | Each segment properly aligned |
| 4 | 0x4 | Segment unmapped |
| 8 | 0x8 | Next segment unmapped (mate unmapped) |
| 16 | 0x10 | SEQ reverse complemented |
| 32 | 0x20 | SEQ of next segment reversed |
| 64 | 0x40 | First segment in template (read 1) |
| 128 | 0x80 | Last segment in template (read 2) |
| 256 | 0x100 | Secondary alignment |
| 512 | 0x200 | Not passing filters (QC fail) |
| 1024 | 0x400 | PCR or optical duplicate |
| 2048 | 0x800 | Supplementary alignment |

**Common Filter Combinations:**
- `-F 4` → Keep only mapped reads
- `-f 2` → Keep only properly paired reads
- `-F 1796` → Exclude unmapped + secondary + QC-fail + duplicates (standard for variant calling)

### 2.4 Index File Types

| Index | Extension | BAM Size Limit | Use Case |
|---|---|---|---|
| BAI | `.bam.bai` | ≤512 Mb chromosomes | Standard human/model organism BAM |
| CSI | `.bam.csi` | Unlimited | Large scaffolds, plant genomes, long reads |
| CRAI | `.cram.crai` | Unlimited | CRAM files |
| FAI | `.fasta.fai` | — | Reference FASTA indexing |

---

## 3. Complete Command Reference

### 3.1 Command Taxonomy

SAMtools ships with **35+ subcommands** organized into five functional categories:

#### 🗂️ Indexing Commands
| Command | Purpose | Key Use |
|---|---|---|
| `dict` | Create `.dict` sequence dictionary for FASTA | Required by GATK |
| `faidx` | Index/extract FASTA or FASTQ by region | Reference indexing, subsequence extraction |
| `fqidx` | Index/extract FASTQ by name | FASTQ random access |
| `index` | Index sorted BAM/CRAM for random access | **Universally required after sort** |

#### ✏️ Editing Commands
| Command | Purpose | Key Use |
|---|---|---|
| `addreplacerg` | Add/replace @RG (read group) tags | Multi-sample GATK prep |
| `ampliconclip` | Clip primer sequences from amplicon reads | Amplicon sequencing workflows |
| `calmd` | Recalculate MD/NM tags; implement BAQ | Improves SNP calling accuracy |
| `fixmate` | Fill mate coordinates, ISIZE, mate flags | **Required upstream of markdup** |
| `markdup` | Mark/remove PCR and optical duplicates | **Standard preprocessing step** |
| `reheader` | Replace BAM/CRAM header (fast, no re-read) | Metadata correction |
| `reset` | Revert aligner changes, restore unmapped state | Re-alignment preparation |
| `targetcut` | Cut fosmid regions | Fosmid pool sequencing |

#### 📁 File Operation Commands
| Command | Purpose | Key Use |
|---|---|---|
| `cat` | Concatenate BAMs (no re-sorting) | Joining lane BAMs with same header |
| `collate` | Group reads by name (faster than sort -n) | Pre-fastq conversion grouping |
| `consensus` | Generate consensus FASTA/FASTQ/pileup | Amplicon/viral assembly |
| `depad` | Convert padded to unpadded BAM (CIGAR P) | Amplicon-specific format handling |
| `import` | Convert FASTQ → unmapped BAM (uBAM) | GATK uBAM workflow initialization |
| `merge` | Merge multiple sorted BAMs | Multi-library/lane merging |
| `mpileup` | Multi-sample pileup generation | **Variant calling foundation** |
| `quickcheck` | Fast file integrity verification | Pipeline QC gate |
| `sort` | Sort by coordinate, name, or tag | **Standard preprocessing step** |
| `split` | Split BAM by tag (default: RG) | Per-sample demultiplexing |

#### 📊 Statistics Commands
| Command | Purpose | Output |
|---|---|---|
| `ampliconstats` | Per-amplicon sequencing statistics | Text stats with amplicon breakdown |
| `bedcov` | Total read depth per BED region | Per-region depth sum |
| `coverage` | Per-reference-sequence depth & % coverage | Summary table |
| `depth` | Per-base read depth | 3-column: chr, pos, depth |
| `flagstat` | Quick FLAG-based statistics | Summary counts (JSON/TSV/text) |
| `idxstats` | Reads mapped/unmapped per chromosome | 4-column table |
| `phase` | Phase heterozygous SNPs | Phased output |
| `stats` | Comprehensive QC statistics | Multi-section report (SN, IS, COV, etc.) |

#### 👁️ Viewing Commands
| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `flags` | Explain FLAG field values in human-readable form |
| `head` | Display BAM/CRAM header |
| `samples` | List sample names from @RG headers across multiple files |
| `tview` | Interactive terminal alignment viewer (ncurses) |
| `view` | **Primary command**: SAM↔BAM↔CRAM conversion + filtering |

---

## 4. Detailed Parameter Documentation

### 4.1 Universal Global Options (All Commands)

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--threads / -@` | `INT` | `0` | Additional worker threads (**total = value + 1**); effective for view/sort/markdup/merge/index |
| `--reference` | `filepath` | none | Reference FASTA (required for CRAM decode/encode) |
| `--input-fmt` | `string` | auto-detect | Force input format: `sam`, `bam`, `cram` |
| `--output-fmt / -O` | `string` | varies | Force output format: `SAM`, `BAM`, `CRAM` |
| `--write-index` | `flag` | off | Auto-write index alongside output (sort/view/merge/markdup) |
| `--verbosity` | `INT` | `1` | Log verbosity level (0–9) |
| `--input-fmt-option` | `OPT[=VAL]` | — | Format-specific input options |
| `--output-fmt-option` | `OPT[=VAL]` | — | Format-specific output options |

> ⚠️ **Threading Note:** `-@ N` adds N **additional** threads. Total threads = N+1. I/O-bound operations have diminishing returns above 8–16 threads. For sort, `4–8` threads with `-m 4G` is typically optimal.

### 4.2 `samtools view` — Format Conversion & Filtering

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description | GenePattern Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `-b / --bam` | flag | off | Output BAM format | High |
| `-C / --cram` | flag | off | Output CRAM format | High |
| `-h / --with-header` | flag | off | Include SAM header in output | High |
| `-H / --header-only` | flag | off | Output header only | Medium |
| `-c / --count` | flag | off | Output count of matching records (integer) | Medium |
| `-o FILE` | filepath | stdout | Output file path | High |
| `-T FILE` | filepath (FASTA) | none | Reference FASTA (required for CRAM I/O) | High |
| `-L FILE` | filepath (.bed) | none | Extract reads overlapping BED regions | High |
| `-r STR` | string | none | Keep only reads in read group STR | Medium |
| `-q / --min-MQ INT` | INT [0–255] | `0` | Minimum MAPQ filter | High |
| `-m INT` | INT | none | Minimum query length filter | Medium |
| `-f / --require-flags FLAG` | INT (bitmask) | `0` | Keep reads with ALL specified FLAG bits set | High |
| `-F / --excl-flags FLAG` | INT (bitmask) | `0` | Exclude reads with ANY specified FLAG bits | High |
| `--rf / --incl-flags FLAG` | INT (bitmask) | `0` | Keep reads with ANY specified FLAG bits | Medium |
| `-e / --expr STR` | string expression | none | Filter expression (e.g., `'[AS] >= 30'`) | Medium |
| `-s / --subsample FLOAT` | FLOAT | `1.0` | Random subsample fraction (seed = integer part) | Low |
| `-@ INT` | INT | `0` | Additional threads | High |
| `-P / --fetch-pairs` | flag | off | Retrieve complete pairs spanning multiple regions | Medium |
| `-M / --use-index` | flag | off | Use multi-region iterator | Medium |
| `--sanitize FLAGS` | flags | off | Fix SAM specification violations | Low |
| `-u` | flag | off | Uncompressed BAM output (streaming) | Medium |

**Usage Examples:**
```bash
# SAM → BAM with header
samtools view -bS -h -o output.bam input.sam

# Extract properly paired, mapped, non-duplicate reads
samtools view -b -f 2 -F 1804 -q 20 -o filtered.bam sorted.bam

# Region extraction
samtools view -b input.bam "chr1:1000000-2000000" -o region.bam

# BAM → CRAM with reference
samtools view -C -T reference.fasta --write-index -o output.cram input.bam

# Count mapped reads
samtools view -c -F 4 input.bam

# Filter by BED target
samtools view -b -L capture_targets.bed input.bam -o targeted.bam
```

### 4.3 `samtools sort` — Alignment Sorting

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `-o FILE` | filepath | stdout | Output file | Required for most uses |
| `-n / --name` | flag | off | Sort by read name (lexicographic) | Required for fixmate, fastq conversion |
| `-t TAG` | string | none | Sort by value of SAM tag | Specialized workflows |
| `--template-coordinate` | flag | off | Sort by 5' template position | Required for some duplicate marking modes |
| `-m INT` | memory string | `768M` | Max memory per thread (K/M/G suffixes) | Increase for large WGS (recommend 4G) |
| `-l INT` | INT [0–9] | `6` | BAM output compression level | 0=none, 9=max |
| `-u` | flag | off | Uncompressed BAM output | For piping |
| `-T PREFIX` | string | system temp | Prefix for temporary files | Use fast scratch disk |
| `-O FORMAT` | string | BAM | Output format: `sam`, `bam`, `cram` | |
| `-@ INT` | INT | `0` | Additional threads | |

```bash
# Standard coordinate sort with 8 threads, 4G per thread
samtools sort -@ 8 -m 4G -o sorted.bam -T /scratch/tmp input.bam

# Name sort for fixmate/fastq pipeline
samtools sort -n -@ 8 -o namesorted.bam input.bam
```

### 4.4 `samtools markdup` — Duplicate Marking

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `-r` | flag | off | **Remove** duplicates (vs. just marking) | Modifies FLAG bit 0x400 |
| `-d INT` | INT | `0` (disabled) | Optical duplicate pixel distance | 100 HiSeq 2500/4000; **2500 NovaSeq** patterned flowcell |
| `-S` | flag | off | Also mark supplementary reads of duplicates | Recommended for GATK |
| `-m STR` | string | `t` | Detection method: `t`=template, `s`=sequence | Template preferred |
| `-s` | flag | off | Print duplicate statistics to stderr | QC monitoring |
| `--json FILE` | filepath | none | Write stats in JSON format | Structured logging |
| `-l INT` | INT | `300` | Max read length (affects temp storage) | Adjust for long reads |
| `-c` | flag | off | Clear previous duplicate tags | Re-processing |
| `--write-index` | flag | off | Auto-write BAI index with output | Convenience |
| `-@ INT` | INT | `0` | Additional threads | |

> ⚠️ **Critical prerequisite:** `markdup` **requires** prior execution of `samtools fixmate -m`. The `-m` flag adds `ms` (mate score) tags that markdup relies upon for template-based detection.

### 4.5 `samtools mpileup` — Pileup Generation

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `-f FILE` | filepath (FASTA) | none | **Required**: faidx-indexed reference genome | Enables BAQ calculation |
| `-q INT` | INT | `0` | Min mapping quality | Recommend **20** for variant calling |
| `-Q INT` | INT | `13` | Min base quality | Recommend **20–30** for variant calling |
| `-d INT` | INT | `8000` | Max per-file depth cap | Increase for amplicon data (e.g., 100000) |
| `-r REGION` | string | all | Restrict to genomic region (chr:start-end) | |
| `-l FILE` | filepath (BED) | none | Restrict to positions/regions in file | |
| `-b FILE` | filepath | none | List of BAM files (one per line) | Multi-sample |
| `-A` | flag | off | Count orphan reads (anomalous pairs) | |
| `-B / --no-BAQ` | flag | off | Disable BAQ; **leave enabled for SNP calling** | |
| `-C INT` | INT | `0` | Adjust MAPQ; recommended **50** for BWA | |
| `-v / --vcf` | flag | off | Generate VCF output | |
| `-u` | flag | off | Uncompressed BCF/VCF | For piping to bcftools |
| `-o FILE` | filepath | stdout | Output file | |

```bash
# Multi-sample BCF for bcftools call
samtools mpileup -f ref.fasta -q 20 -Q 20 -C 50 -d 10000 \
  --output-tags DP,AD -u -b sample_list.txt | \
  bcftools call -mv -Oz -o variants.vcf.gz
```

### 4.6 `samtools stats` — Comprehensive QC

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-r FILE` | filepath (FASTA) | none | Reference; enables GC-depth + mismatch-per-cycle plots |
| `-t FILE` | filepath (BED) | none | Target regions BED (targeted sequencing QC) |
| `-d` | flag | off | Exclude duplicate-marked reads |
| `-D` | flag | off | Remove read-pair overlap from fragment count/coverage |
| `-c MIN,MAX,STEP` | integers | `1,1000,1` | Coverage histogram bin parameters |
| `-i INT` | INT | `8000` | Maximum insert size to consider |
| `-l INT` | INT | `-1` | Filter by read length |
| `-S STR` | string | none | Split stats by `RG` or `SM` (per read group/sample) |
| `-f INT` | INT (bitmask) | `0` | Required FLAG filter |
| `-F INT` | INT (bitmask) | `0` | Excluding FLAG filter |

**Output Sections:**
- `SN` — Summary numbers (total reads, bases, duplicates, mapped %, etc.)
- `FFQ/LFQ` — Per-cycle first/last fragment quality
- `IS` — Insert size histogram
- `COV` — Coverage histogram
- `GCD` — GC content vs. depth (requires `-r`)
- `IC` — Indels per cycle

### 4.7 `samtools depth` — Per-base Coverage

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-a` | flag | off | Output all positions (including zero depth) |
| `-aa` | flag | off | Output ALL positions including unused references |
| `-q INT` | INT | `0` | Min base quality threshold |
| `-Q INT` | INT | `0` | Min mapping quality threshold |
| `-m INT` | INT | `8000` | Maximum depth cap |
| `-r REGION` | string | all | Restrict to region |
| `-b FILE` | filepath (BED) | none | Positions/regions from BED file |
| `-H` | flag | off | Print column header |
| `-o FILE` | filepath | stdout | Output file |

### 4.8 `samtools faidx` — FASTA Indexing & Extraction

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| (positional) `region1...` | string | all | Regions to extract (chr:start-end, 1-based) |
| `-n INT` | INT | `60` | FASTA line length in output |
| `-o FILE` | filepath | stdout | Output file |
| `-r FILE` | filepath | none | Read regions from file |
| `-i` | flag | off | Output reverse complement |
| `--fai-idx FILE` | filepath | auto | Read/write FAI index from specific file |

## 5. Installation & Dependencies

### 5.1 Core Dependencies

| Dependency | Required? | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **htslib** (same version) | ✅ Required | SAM/BAM/CRAM I/O, BGZF, thread pool, tabix |
| **zlib** | ✅ Required | Basic gzip compression for BAM |
| **libbz2** | ⚠️ Strongly Recommended | bzip2 support for CRAM compression |
| **liblzma** | ⚠️ Strongly Recommended | XZ/LZMA support for CRAM compression |
| **libdeflate** | 🚀 Optional (Performance) | 20–30% faster BAM compression; highly recommended |
| **libcurl** | 🌐 Optional | HTTP/HTTPS/FTP/S3/GCS remote file access |
| **OpenSSL/GnuTLS** | 🔒 Optional | HTTPS certificate validation |
| **ncurses** | 👁️ Optional | `samtools tview` interactive terminal viewer |

### 5.2 Installation Methods

#### Bioconda (Recommended for GenePattern)
```bash
# Create dedicated environment
conda create -n samtools_env -c bioconda -c conda-forge samtools=1.21
conda activate samtools_env

# Or with mamba (faster)
mamba install -c bioconda -c conda-forge samtools=1.21
```

#### Docker / Singularity (Recommended for GenePattern Modules)
```bash
# BioContainers (official, versioned)
docker pull quay.io/biocontainers/samtools:1.23--h96c455f_0

# StaPH-B maintained images
docker pull staphb/samtools:1.21

# Singularity
singularity pull docker://quay.io/biocontainers/samtools:1.21--h50ea8bc_0
```

#### Build from Source
```bash
# Download official release tarball (includes htslib)
wget https://github.com/samtools/samtools/releases/download/1.21/samtools-1.21.tar.bz2
tar -xjf samtools-1.21.tar.bz2
cd samtools-1.21

# Configure with recommended options
./configure --prefix=/usr/local \
            --with-htslib=. \
            --enable-plugins

make -j$(nproc)
make install
```

#### Package Managers
```bash
apt-get install samtools          # Ubuntu/Debian (may not be latest)
brew install samtools              # macOS Homebrew
module load samtools/1.21          # HPC environment modules
```

### 5.3 System Requirements

| Resource | Minimum | Recommended | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **OS** | Linux/macOS/WSL2 | Linux x86_64 | Windows via WSL2 only |
| **Architecture** | x86_64 | x86_64 / ARM64 | ARM64 via native conda or Rosetta |
| **RAM** | 1 GB | 8–32 GB | Large WGS sorting requires 16–32 GB |
| **Disk (temp)** | Input size | 3x input size | sort creates temp files |
| **Threads** | 1 | 4–16 | Diminishing returns >16 for I/O-bound ops |

---

## 6. Common Workflows & Use Cases

### 6.1 Workflow 1: Standard DNA-seq Preprocessing (GATK Best Practices)

```bash
# Step 1: Align + sort in one pipeline
bwa mem -R "@RG\tID:sample1\tSM:sample1\tPL:ILLUMINA\tLB:lib1" \
    ref.fasta R1.fastq.gz R2.fastq.gz | \
    samtools sort -@ 8 -m 4G -o sorted.bam

# Step 2: Name-sort for fixmate
samtools sort -n -@ 8 -o namesorted.bam sorted.bam

# Step 3: Fix mate information (adds ms/MC tags)
samtools fixmate -m -@ 8 namesorted.bam fixmate.bam

# Step 4: Coordinate sort again
samtools sort -@ 8 -m 4G -o coord_sorted.bam fixmate.bam

# Step 5: Mark/remove duplicates (optical dist=2500 for NovaSeq)
samtools markdup -r -d 2500 -S -s --json markdup_stats.json \
    --write-index -@ 8 coord_sorted.bam dedup.bam

# Step 6: QC reports
samtools flagstat -O json dedup.bam > flagstat.json
samtools stats -r ref.fasta dedup.bam > stats.txt
samtools idxstats dedup.bam > idxstats.txt

# Downstream: GATK HaplotypeCaller, base recalibration, etc.
```

### 6.2 Workflow 2: RNA-seq Post-processing

```bash
# STAR output is already sorted by coordinate
samtools index -@ 8 Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam

# QC statistics
samtools flagstat Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam > flagstat.txt
samtools idxstats Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam > idxstats.txt
samtools stats Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam > stats.txt
samtools coverage Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam > coverage_summary.txt

# Extract reads mapping to specific chromosomes
samtools view -b Aligned.sortedByCoord.out.bam chr1 chr2 chr3 -o subset.bam

# Downstream: featureCounts, StringTie, DESeq2
```

### 6.3 Workflow 3: Format Conversion & Storage Optimization

```bash
# SAM → BAM (during alignment pipeline)
samtools view -bS -@ 4 -o output.bam input.sam

# BAM → CRAM (~40-50% storage savings; requires reference)
samtools view -C -T reference.fasta \
    --output-fmt-option version=3.0 \
    --write-index -@ 4 -o output.cram input.bam

# CRAM → BAM (for tools not supporting CRAM)
samtools view -b -T reference.fasta -o output.bam input.cram

# BAM → FASTQ (for re-alignment)
samtools collate -@ 4 input.bam | \
    samtools fastq -@ 4 -1 R1.fastq.gz -2 R2.fastq.gz \
                   -0 other.fastq.gz -s singleton.fastq.gz
```

### 6.4 Workflow 4: Read Filtering & Extraction

```bash
# Keep only high-quality, properly paired, non-duplicate reads
# FLAG 1804 = 4 (unmapped) + 8 (mate unmapped) + 256 (secondary) + 512 (QC fail) + 1024 (dup)
samtools view -b -f 2 -F 1804 -q 20 -o filtered.bam sorted.bam

# Extract reads from target regions
samtools view -b -L capture_targets.bed sorted.bam -o on_target.bam

# Extract unmapped reads (for pathogen detection)
samtools view -b -f 4 -F 264 sorted.bam -o unmapped.bam

# Random subsample 10% of reads (seed=42)
samtools view -b -s 42.1 sorted.bam -o 10pct_subsample.bam
```

### 6.5 Workflow 5: Multi-sample Pileup & Variant Calling Prep

```bash
# Create sample list file
ls *.dedup.bam > bam_list.txt

# Generate multi-sample BCF for variant calling
samtools mpileup -f ref.fasta \
    -q 20 -Q 20 -C 50 \
    -d 10000 \
    --output-tags DP,AD \
    -u -b bam_list.txt | \
    bcftools call -mv --threads 8 -Oz -o variants.vcf.gz

bcftools index variants.vcf.gz
```

### 6.6 Workflow 6: Coverage Analysis

```bash
# Per-base depth (all positions)
samtools depth -a -Q 20 -q 20 -o depth_per_base.txt sorted.bam

# Coverage summary per chromosome
samtools coverage sorted.bam > chromosome_coverage.txt

# Read depth per target BED region
samtools bedcov capture_targets.bed sorted.bam > region_depth.txt

# Comprehensive QC (feeds MultiQC)
samtools stats -r ref.fasta -d -t capture_targets.bed sorted.bam > bamstats.txt
```

### 6.7 Workflow 7: Amplicon Sequencing

```bash
# Clip primer sequences from reads using primer BED
samtools ampliconclip -b primers.bed --hard-clip \
    -o clipped.bam -r clip_stats.txt input.bam

# Generate amplicon-specific statistics
samtools ampliconstats -F QCFAIL,DUP primers.bed clipped.bam > amplicon_stats.txt

# Plot amplicon coverage (if matplotlib available)
plot-ampliconstats amplicon_stats.txt
```

---

## 7. Comparative Analysis

### 7.1 Tool Comparison Matrix

| Feature | **samtools** | Picard | sambamba | bamtools | GATK | deeptools |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Language** | C | Java | D | C++ | Java | Python |
| **Speed** | ⚡⚡⚡⚡ | ⚡⚡ | ⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡ | ⚡⚡⚡ | ⚡⚡ | ⚡⚡ |
| **Memory footprint** | Low | High (JVM) | Low | Low | High (JVM) | Medium |
| **BAM/SAM/CRAM** | ✅ All three | ✅ BAM/SAM | ✅ BAM/SAM | ✅ BAM/SAM | ✅ BAM/SAM | ✅ BAM |
| **Format conversion** | ✅ Full | ✅ Full | Partial | Partial | Limited | Limited |
| **Sorting** | ✅ | ✅ (SortSam) | ✅ Faster | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| **Deduplication** | ✅ markdup | ✅ MarkDuplicates | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (via Picard) | ❌ |
| **Dup metrics** | Basic | ✅ Detailed | Basic | ❌ | ✅ Detailed | ❌ |
| **Pileup generation** | ✅ mpileup | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| **Coverage depth** | ✅ depth/coverage | ✅ Metrics | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ bamCoverage |
| **Normalization** | ❌ | Limited | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ Full |
| **FASTA indexing** | ✅ faidx | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| **Terminal viewer** | ✅ tview | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Cloud/remote files** | ✅ (libcurl) | ✅ | Limited | ❌ | ✅ | Limited |
| **Streaming pipes** | ✅ Excellent | Limited | Partial | Partial | Limited | ❌ |
| **HPC availability** | ✅ Universal | ✅ Common | ✅ Common | Partial | ✅ Common | ✅ Common |
| **License** | MIT | MIT | GPLv2 | MIT | MIT | MIT |

### 7.2 samtools Unique Strengths

1. **Reference implementation**: Co-authored the SAM/BAM/CRAM specification; ultimate authority
2. **Ubiquity**: Installed on essentially every HPC cluster and bioinformatics server worldwide
3. **Streaming pipeline support**: Designed for Unix pipe chaining; minimal memory overhead
4. **CRAM support**: Native, full-featured CRAM v2.1/3.0/3.1 read/write
5. **mpileup**: The standard multi-sample pileup engine; used by bcftools for population variant calling
6. **tview**: Only major tool with a terminal-based alignment viewer (no GUI required)
7. **Active development**: Frequent releases, responsive maintainers (Wellcome Sanger)
8. **Complete format coverage**: SAM+BAM+CRAM+FASTA+FASTQ+BED+VCF all handled natively

### 7.3 Known Limitations

- **BAI index**: Cannot index chromosomes >512 Mb (use CSI: `samtools index -c`)
- **Duplicate metrics**: Less detailed than Picard's `CollectDuplicateMetrics` (but `--json` flag helps)
- **Thread scaling**: Sort threading less efficient than sambamba at very high core counts (>16)
- **Variant calling**: mpileup output should be piped to bcftools call; not a standalone caller
- **Visualization**: tview is text-only; IGV or deeptools needed for publication figures
- **No BAM validation**: Unlike Picard, does not perform exhaustive format validation by default

---

## 8. Scientific Literature & Adoption

### 8.1 Primary Citations

1. **Li H, Handsaker B, Wysoker A, et al.** (2009). The Sequence Alignment/Map format and SAMtools. *Bioinformatics*, **25(16)**, 2078–2079. DOI: [10.1093/bioinformatics/btp352](https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btp352). PMID: **19505943**
   - **~40,000+ citations** (Google Scholar) — one of the most cited bioinformatics papers in history

2. **Danecek P, Bonfield JK, Liddle J, et al.** (2021). Twelve years of SAMtools and BCFtools. *GigaScience*, **10(2)**, giab008. DOI: [10.1093/gigascience/giab008](https://doi.org/10.1093/gigascience/giab008)
   - Comprehensive review of the tool evolution, new features, CRAM support, performance improvements

3. **Li H** (2011). A statistical framework for SNP calling, mutation discovery, association mapping and population genetical parameter estimation from sequencing data. *Bioinformatics*, **27(21)**, 2987–2993. DOI: [10.1093/bioinformatics/btr509](https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btr509)
   - Describes the BAQ (Base Alignment Quality) and mpileup statistical framework

### 8.2 Adoption in Major Projects

| Project / Pipeline | SAMtools Role |
|---|---|
| **1000 Genomes Project** | Standard data processing tool (original use case) |
| **ENCODE Project** | Required tool in BAM processing specification |
| **TCGA / GDC Pipeline** | Standard alignment preprocessing |
| **GTEx Project** | Core RNA-seq BAM processing |
| **nf-core pipelines** | Core component of all pipelines (nf-core/sarek, nf-core/rnaseq, etc.) |
| **GATK Best Practices** | Sorting, indexing, and deduplication step |
| **Galaxy Platform** | Multiple wrapped tools (samtools_sort, samtools_flagstat, etc.) |
| **Snakemake Wrappers** | Official wrappers for all major subcommands |

---

## 9. GenePattern Integration Recommendations

### 9.1 Module Architecture Strategy

Given samtools' wide command set, we strongly recommend a **multi-module approach** rather than one monolithic module:

| Proposed Module Name | Subcommand(s) | Priority | Use Case |
|---|---|---|---|
| `samtools.View` | `view` | 🔴 Critical | SAM/BAM/CRAM conversion + filtering |
| `samtools.Sort` | `sort` | 🔴 Critical | Coordinate/name sorting |
| `samtools.Index` | `index` | 🔴 Critical | BAM/CRAM indexing |
| `samtools.Flagstat` | `flagstat` | 🔴 Critical | Quick QC statistics |
| `samtools.Stats` | `stats` | 🟠 High | Comprehensive QC report |
| `samtools.Markdup` | `fixmate` + `markdup` | 🟠 High | Duplicate marking pipeline |
| `samtools.Depth` | `depth` / `coverage` | 🟠 High | Coverage analysis |
| `samtools.Mpileup` | `mpileup` | 🟡 Medium | Variant calling preparation |
| `samtools.Merge` | `merge` | 🟡 Medium | Multi-BAM merging |
| `samtools.Faidx` | `faidx` | 🟡 Medium | FASTA reference indexing |
| `samtools.Convert` | `fastq` / `fasta` / `import` | 🟢 Low | Format interconversion |

### 9.2 Containerization

```dockerfile
# Recommended Docker base for GenePattern module
FROM quay.io/biocontainers/samtools:1.23--h96c455f_0

# Or build from bioconda for reproducibility:
FROM continuumio/miniconda3:latest
RUN conda install -c bioconda -c conda-forge samtools=1.21 --yes \
    && conda clean -afy
```

### 9.3 Parameter UI Recommendations

#### For `samtools.View` Module:
```
Input Parameters:
├── input_file          [FILE]     Input BAM/SAM/CRAM file (required)
├── output_format       [ENUM]     Output format: BAM | SAM | CRAM (default: BAM)
├── output_filename     [TEXT]     Output file name (default: output.bam)
├── reference_genome    [FILE]     Reference FASTA (required for CRAM I/O)
├── region              [TEXT]     Genomic region (e.g., chr1:1000-5000; optional)
├── target_bed          [FILE]     BED file of target regions (optional)
├── min_mapping_quality [INT]      Min MAPQ filter (default: 0, range: 0-255)
├── require_flags       [INT]      Required FLAG bits (default: 0)
├── exclude_flags       [INT]      Excluded FLAG bits (default: 0)
├── include_header      [BOOL]     Include SAM header (default: true)
├── threads             [INT]      Number of threads (default: 1, max: 16)
└── advanced_expression [TEXT]     Filter expression (optional)
```

#### For `samtools.Sort` Module:
```
Input Parameters:
├── input_file          [FILE]     Input BAM/CRAM file (required)
├── sort_order          [ENUM]     coordinate | name | template-coordinate (default: coordinate)
├── output_filename     [TEXT]     Output file name (default: sorted.bam)
├── memory_per_thread   [TEXT]     Memory per thread: 512M | 1G | 2G | 4G | 8G (default: 768M)
├── compression_level   [INT]      Compression 0-9 (default: 6)
├── threads             [INT]      Number of threads (default: 1)
├── temp_directory      [TEXT]     Temporary file prefix (optional)
└── write_index         [BOOL]     Auto-write index file (default: true)
```

#### For `samtools.Flagstat` Module:
```
Input Parameters:
├── input_file          [FILE]     Input BAM/SAM/CRAM file (required)
├── output_format       [ENUM]     default | json | tsv (default: json)
├── threads             [INT]      Number of threads (default: 1)
└── output_filename     [TEXT]     Output file name (default: flagstat.json)
```

### 9.4 Critical Integration Considerations

1. **Index File Co-location**: BAM `.bai` index must reside in the same directory as the BAM file. GenePattern file handling must ensure this. Use `--write-index` flag or pair commands appropriately.

2. **Reference Genome Handling**: CRAM operations require a reference genome. This should be a separate, versioned input parameter. Consider linking to commonly used assemblies (GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, mm10, mm39).

3. **Threading**: GenePattern job resources should expose thread count parameter and map it to `-@` (note: `-@ N` = N *additional* threads, so total = N+1).

4. **Temporary File Space**: `samtools sort` creates temporary files of up to 3x input size. GenePattern jobs need sufficient scratch space; use `-T` to specify scratch path.

5. **Streaming Pipelines**: For complex pipelines (e.g., align → sort → markdup), consider a combined workflow module to avoid writing large intermediate files.

6. **Output Naming Convention**: Establish consistent naming: `{sample}.sorted.bam`, `{sample}.dedup.bam`, `{sample}.flagstat.json`, etc.

7. **Error Handling**: samtools exits with code 1 on errors and prints to stderr. GenePattern wrapper should capture stderr and surface it in job logs.

8. **GenePattern Existing Context**: GenePattern already references SAMtools in RNA-seq documentation and has Picard wrappers. samtools modules should complement (not duplicate) Picard functionality — use samtools for speed-sensitive operations (sort, view, index) and Picard for detailed metrics.

9. **Docker Image Versioning**: Pin exact image digest (not just tag) for reproducibility: `quay.io/biocontainers/samtools:1.21--h50ea8bc_0@sha256:...`

10. **CRAM Default Change Warning**: htslib ≥1.21 defaults to CRAM v3.1 output. Some downstream tools may not support v3.1. Use `--output-fmt-option version=3.0` for maximum compatibility, or document version behavior clearly.

# Comprehensive GenePattern Module Plan: Samtools Suite (samtools v1.23.1)

## Executive Summary

This plan describes a **multi-module suite** approach for wrapping samtools v1.23.1 in GenePattern. Given samtools' 35+ subcommands spanning fundamentally different operations (format conversion, sorting, QC statistics, coverage analysis, variant calling preparation), a monolithic module would be unusable. Instead, we propose **8 focused modules** organized as a GenePattern module suite under the "Samtools" namespace. Each module wraps one primary subcommand, uses a dedicated bash wrapper script, and shares a common Docker image.

**Suite Members:**
1. Samtools.View — Format conversion and read filtering
2. Samtools.Sort — Coordinate/name/template sorting
3. Samtools.Index — BAM/CRAM index generation
4. Samtools.Flagstat — FLAG-based QC statistics
5. Samtools.Stats — Comprehensive QC metrics
6. Samtools.Markdup — Duplicate marking and removal
7. Samtools.Depth — Per-base read depth
8. Samtools.Mpileup — Multi-sample pileup for variant calling

---

## Module 1: Samtools.View

### Purpose
Primary format conversion (SAM↔BAM↔CRAM) and read filtering hub for NGS workflows.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_wrapper.sh
The bash wrapper script parses named parameters from the GenePattern command line and constructs the appropriate samtools view invocation. It handles:
- Converting output format choice (BAM/SAM/CRAM) to the correct flag (-b/-C or -O)
- Conditionally adding -T reference flag when reference.genome is non-empty
- Conditionally adding -L target.bed flag when target.bed is non-empty
- Conditionally adding -r region flag when region is non-empty
- Mapping include.header boolean to -h flag
- Adding --write-index for BAM/CRAM output to auto-generate .bai/.crai files
- Setting -@ threads appropriately
- Constructing -q MAPQ, -f require.flags, -F exclude.flags arguments

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --output.format <output.format> --output.filename <output.filename> <reference.genome> <region> <target.bed> --min.mapping.quality <min.mapping.quality> --require.flags <require.flags> --exclude.flags <exclude.flags> --include.header <include.header> --threads <threads>
```

### Parameter Groups
**Required Inputs:**
- input.file (File, SAM/BAM/CRAM)

**Output Configuration:**
- output.format (Choice: BAM/SAM/CRAM)
- output.filename (Text)

**Optional Inputs:**
- reference.genome (File, FASTA) — required for CRAM I/O
- region (Text) — genomic region filter
- target.bed (File, BED) — targeted region filter

**Read Filtering:**
- min.mapping.quality (Integer, default 0)
- require.flags (Integer, default 0)
- exclude.flags (Integer, default 0)
- include.header (Choice Yes/No, default Yes)

**Performance:**
- threads (Integer, default 4)

### Output Files
- {output.filename} — converted/filtered alignment file
- {output.filename}.bai or .crai — auto-generated index (when output is BAM/CRAM)

---

## Module 2: Samtools.Sort

### Purpose
Sorts alignment files by genomic coordinate (default), read name, or template coordinate. Required before indexing and many downstream tools.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_sort_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- Mapping sort.order choice to -n (name), --template-coordinate, or default (coordinate)
- Converting memory.per.thread string (e.g., "4G") to -m flag
- Setting compression level -l
- Adding --write-index for automatic index creation
- Thread management with -@
- Temp file prefix handling via -T

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_sort_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --sort.order <sort.order> --output.filename <output.filename> --memory.per.thread <memory.per.thread> --compression.level <compression.level> --threads <threads> --write.index <write.index>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, BAM/SAM/CRAM, required)
- sort.order (Choice: coordinate/name/template-coordinate, default: coordinate)
- output.filename (Text, default: sorted.bam)
- memory.per.thread (Choice: 512M/1G/2G/4G/8G, default: 4G)
- compression.level (Integer 0-9, default: 6)
- threads (Integer, default: 4)
- write.index (Choice Yes/No, default: Yes)

---

## Module 3: Samtools.Index

### Purpose
Creates index files (.bai, .csi, .crai) for sorted BAM/CRAM files, enabling random access by genomic coordinate.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_index_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- Generating .bai (BAI), .csi (CSI), or auto-detected index from index.type choice
- Passing -@ threads
- Outputting index file alongside input BAM/CRAM
- Warning when CSI is needed for large chromosomes (>512 Mb)

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_index_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --index.type <index.type> --threads <threads>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, BAM/CRAM, required)
- index.type (Choice: BAI/CSI/auto, default: BAI)
- threads (Integer, default: 4)

### Output Files
- {input.file}.bai — BAI index
- {input.file}.csi — CSI index (if selected)
- {input.file}.crai — CRAI index (CRAM files)

---

## Module 4: Samtools.Flagstat

### Purpose
Generates quick summary statistics based on SAM FLAG field values. The fastest QC check for any alignment file.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_flagstat_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- Mapping output.format choice to -O flag (default/json/tsv)
- Setting output filename
- Thread management

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_flagstat_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --output.format <output.format> --output.filename <output.filename> --threads <threads>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, SAM/BAM/CRAM, required)
- output.format (Choice: text/json/tsv, default: json)
- output.filename (Text, default: flagstat.json)
- threads (Integer, default: 4)

### Output Fields (samtools flagstat JSON)
Total reads, secondary, supplementary, duplicates, mapped, paired, read1, read2, properly paired, with itself and mate mapped, singletons, mate on different chromosome, QC-passed/failed counts for each.

---

## Module 5: Samtools.Stats

### Purpose
Comprehensive BAM QC statistics covering read counts, base quality, insert size distribution, GC content, coverage histograms, and per-cycle quality metrics. Compatible with MultiQC.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_stats_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- Optional -r reference for GC-depth plots
- Optional -t target BED for targeted sequencing metrics
- -d flag for excluding duplicates
- -S split-by option (RG or SM)
- Thread management

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_stats_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> <reference.genome> <target.bed> --exclude.duplicates <exclude.duplicates> <split.by> --output.filename <output.filename> --threads <threads>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, SAM/BAM/CRAM, required)
- reference.genome (File, FASTA, optional — enables GC-depth plots)
- target.bed (File, BED, optional — targeted sequencing QC)
- exclude.duplicates (Choice Yes/No, default: No)
- split.by (Choice: none/RG/SM, optional — per-read-group stats)
- output.filename (Text, default: stats.txt)
- threads (Integer, default: 4)

---

## Module 6: Samtools.Markdup

### Purpose
Marks (and optionally removes) PCR and optical duplicate reads. Critical preprocessing step for variant calling workflows. Implements the samtools fixmate→markdup pipeline internally.

### Important Design Decision
The Samtools.Markdup module wraps BOTH `samtools fixmate` AND `samtools markdup` as an integrated pipeline:
1. fixmate -m (adds mate score ms tags — required by markdup)
2. Name-sort → fixmate → coordinate-sort → markdup are all handled internally
This avoids exposing complex intermediate steps to the user.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_markdup_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- Running name-sort → fixmate -m → coord-sort → markdup pipeline
- -r flag for removing duplicates
- -d INT for optical duplicate pixel distance (100 for HiSeq, 2500 for NovaSeq patterned flowcells)
- -S flag for marking supplementary reads
- --json stats output
- Thread management throughout pipeline

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_markdup_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --remove.duplicates <remove.duplicates> --optical.distance <optical.distance> --mark.supplementary <mark.supplementary> --output.filename <output.filename> --stats.filename <stats.filename> --threads <threads>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, BAM/CRAM, required — should be coordinate-sorted)
- remove.duplicates (Choice Yes/No, default: No — mark only)
- optical.distance (Choice: disabled/100/2500, default: disabled — set to 100 for HiSeq 2500/4000, 2500 for NovaSeq patterned flowcells)
- mark.supplementary (Choice Yes/No, default: Yes — recommended for GATK)
- output.filename (Text, default: markdup.bam)
- stats.filename (Text, default: markdup_stats.json)
- threads (Integer, default: 4)

---

## Module 7: Samtools.Depth

### Purpose
Generates per-base read depth (coverage) output for visualization, quality assessment, and coverage analysis. Outputs a 3-column TSV: chromosome, position, depth.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_depth_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- -a flag for outputting zero-coverage positions
- -q base quality filter
- -Q mapping quality filter
- -m max depth cap
- Optional -r region restriction
- Optional -b BED file restriction
- Output file handling

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_depth_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --output.all.positions <output.all.positions> --min.base.quality <min.base.quality> --min.mapping.quality <min.mapping.quality> --max.depth <max.depth> <region> <target.bed> --output.filename <output.filename>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, BAM/CRAM, required)
- output.all.positions (Choice Yes/No, default: No — if Yes, outputs zero-depth positions)
- min.base.quality (Integer, default: 0)
- min.mapping.quality (Integer, default: 0)
- max.depth (Integer, default: 8000)
- region (Text, optional — genomic region restriction)
- target.bed (File, BED, optional — restrict to BED regions)
- output.filename (Text, default: depth.txt)

---

## Module 8: Samtools.Mpileup

### Purpose
Generates multi-sample pileup format output for variant calling preparation. Standard upstream step for bcftools call. Supports BAQ calculation, MAPQ/BQSR adjustments, and per-position statistics.

### Wrapper Script: samtools_mpileup_wrapper.sh
Handles:
- -f reference (required for BAQ)
- -q MAPQ and -Q base quality thresholds
- -d depth cap
- Optional -r region and -l BED file
- -v flag for VCF output vs. pileup text
- -C 50 MAPQ adjustment for BWA alignments
- Output format and file handling

### Command Line
```
bash <libdir>samtools_mpileup_wrapper.sh --input.file <input.file> --reference.genome <reference.genome> --min.mapping.quality <min.mapping.quality> --min.base.quality <min.base.quality> --max.depth <max.depth> <region> <target.bed> --output.vcf <output.vcf> --output.filename <output.filename>
```

### Parameters
- input.file (File, BAM/CRAM, required)
- reference.genome (File, FASTA, required — for BAQ calculation)
- min.mapping.quality (Integer, default: 20)
- min.base.quality (Integer, default: 20)
- max.depth (Integer, default: 8000)
- region (Text, optional)
- target.bed (File, BED, optional)
- output.vcf (Choice Yes/No, default: No — if Yes produces VCF instead of pileup)
- output.filename (Text, default: mpileup.txt or mpileup.vcf)

---

## Docker & Infrastructure

### Docker Image
- Base: quay.io/biocontainers/samtools:1.23--h96c455f_0
- All 8 modules share a single Docker image
- Module-specific wrapper scripts are bundled in the libdir
- Image includes: samtools 1.23.1, htslib, all compression libraries (zlib, bzip2, lzma, libdeflate)

### Resource Requirements
| Module | CPU | Memory | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Samtools.View | 8 | 8GB | I/O-bound; benefits from threads |
| Samtools.Sort | 8 | 32GB | Large temp disk (3x input); memory-intensive |
| Samtools.Index | 4 | 4GB | RAM-light but CPU-moderate |
| Samtools.Flagstat | 4 | 4GB | Very fast; minimal resources |
| Samtools.Stats | 4 | 8GB | I/O and computation |
| Samtools.Markdup | 8 | 16GB | Internal pipeline; multi-step |
| Samtools.Depth | 4 | 8GB | Per-base output can be large |
| Samtools.Mpileup | 8 | 16GB | Multi-file pileup; compute-intensive |

---

## Wrapper Script Design Pattern

All wrapper scripts follow this pattern:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Parse named arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --input.file) INPUT_FILE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --output.format) OUTPUT_FORMAT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        # ... other params
        *) shift ;;
    esac
done

# Validate required parameters
if [[ -z "${INPUT_FILE:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: input.file is required" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Build command
CMD="samtools view"

# Handle output format
case "${OUTPUT_FORMAT}" in
    BAM) CMD="$CMD -b" ;;
    SAM) CMD="$CMD" ;;
    CRAM) CMD="$CMD -C" ;;
esac

# Handle optional parameters (check for empty string)
if [[ -n "${REFERENCE_GENOME:-}" ]]; then
    CMD="$CMD -T ${REFERENCE_GENOME}"
fi

# Include header
if [[ "${INCLUDE_HEADER}" == "true" ]]; then
    CMD="$CMD -h"
fi

# Add threading
CMD="$CMD -@ ${THREADS}"

# Add filtering
CMD="$CMD -q ${MIN_MAPPING_QUALITY} -f ${REQUIRE_FLAGS} -F ${EXCLUDE_FLAGS}"

# Add output
CMD="$CMD -o ${OUTPUT_FILENAME}"

# Add input
CMD="$CMD ${INPUT_FILE}"

# Add write-index for indexed formats
if [[ "${OUTPUT_FORMAT}" != "SAM" ]]; then
    CMD="$CMD --write-index"
fi

echo "Running: $CMD"
eval "$CMD"
```

---

## Validation & Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests (per module)
1. **Format conversion**: SAM→BAM, BAM→SAM, BAM→CRAM, CRAM→BAM
2. **FLAG filtering**: Test -f 2, -F 4, -F 1796 with known outputs
3. **MAPQ filtering**: Verify correct reads excluded at threshold
4. **Region extraction**: Test chr:start-end format
5. **BED filtering**: Verify BED-based extraction
6. **Index generation**: Verify .bai/.csi/.crai created correctly
7. **Sort order**: Verify coordinate and name sort correctness
8. **Markdup pipeline**: Verify duplicate FLAG (0x400) marking
9. **Stats output**: Verify SN/IS/COV sections present
10. **Depth output**: Verify 3-column format, zero positions

### Integration Tests
1. **Full GATK workflow**: BWA output → Sort → Index → Markdup → Flagstat → Stats
2. **RNA-seq workflow**: STAR output → Index → Flagstat → Stats → Coverage
3. **BAM → CRAM compression**: Verify lossless round-trip with MD5 checksums
4. **Empty input handling**: Verify graceful failure with appropriate error message
5. **Large file handling**: Test with WGS BAM (30-80 GB)
6. **Multi-threading**: Verify output consistency across different thread counts

### Test Data
- NA12878 WGS subset (chromosome 20 or 22)
- Synthetic BAM with known FLAG patterns
- Small SAM with all possible FLAG combinations
- CRAM test file with reference

---

## Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1 (Critical Priority — Week 1-2)
1. Samtools.View — Foundation; most versatile module
2. Samtools.Sort — Required by virtually all pipelines
3. Samtools.Index — Required for all random-access operations
4. Samtools.Flagstat — Quick QC; very simple implementation

### Phase 2 (High Priority — Week 3-4)
5. Samtools.Stats — Comprehensive QC; MultiQC compatible
6. Samtools.Markdup — GATK pipeline integration

### Phase 3 (Medium Priority — Week 5-6)
7. Samtools.Depth — Coverage analysis
8. Samtools.Mpileup — Variant calling preparation

### Post-Launch Considerations
- Samtools.Merge — Multi-BAM merging
- Samtools.Faidx — Reference FASTA indexing
- Samtools.Convert — FASTQ/FASTA conversion
- Pipeline module: Samtools.Preprocess (Sort → Index → Markdup → Flagstat combined)

---

## GenePattern Integration Notes

1. **Index co-location**: BAM .bai files MUST be in the same directory as the BAM. GenePattern file staging must handle this; use --write-index flag consistently.

2. **Reference genome registry**: Consider a reference genome dropdown linked to GenePattern's genome registry (GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, mm10, mm39, etc.) for CRAM operations.

3. **Threading convention**: -@ N means N ADDITIONAL threads. All wrappers set -@ (threads-1) when mapping to total thread count, OR simply pass threads directly as additional threads to be conservative.

4. **Error handling**: All wrappers set `set -euo pipefail` and capture stderr to GenePattern job logs.

5. **Temp disk for Sort**: Samtools.Sort creates up to 3x input size in temp files. GenePattern job scheduler must allocate sufficient scratch disk.

6. **MultiQC compatibility**: Samtools.Stats output (bamstats.txt) is directly parseable by MultiQC for report generation in QC workflows.

7. **CRAM version note**: samtools 1.21+ defaults to CRAM v3.1. For maximum downstream compatibility, wrapper should offer a CRAM version choice option or default to v3.0 with documentation.
